stories.title: House rebukes Trump in vote to restrict his Iran war powers
stories.summary: The US House of Representatives voted 214 to 208 to restrict President Trump's war powers in Iran, with four Republicans joining Democrats in support of the measure. This rebuke comes as the conflict in Iran escalates, following a recent ceasefire. The resolution, however, lacks the force of law and would not compel the administration to end the war.

stories.title: Sebastian Berhalter finalizing transfer to Middlesbrough from Vancouver: Sources
stories.summary: Sebastian Berhalter, a 25-year-old U.S. national team midfielder, is finalizing a transfer to Middlesbrough from the Vancouver Whitecaps for a fee in the region of $2 million. Berhalter, who appeared in all five U.S. matches at the World Cup, will join the Championship team during the summer transfer window. He will reunite with former Columbus Crew teammate Aidan Morris, another U.S. national team midfielder, at Middlesbrough.

stories.title: Richard Carapaz's late attack secures victory in Stage 18 of the 2026 Tour de France
stories.summary: Richard Carapaz secured his first stage win of the 2026 Tour de France in Stage 18, covering 185.2 kilometers from Voiron to Orcières-Merlette. The Ecuadorian cyclist launched a decisive attack in the final stretch to claim the summit and stage victory. Carapaz's win narrows the overall general classification, setting the stage for a thrilling conclusion to the 2026 Tour de France.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France evacuated 20,000 people in the Gironde region on Thursday as wildfires scorched 3,400 hectares near the Bay of Arcachon. Authorities moved 8,800 more residents as a precaution while 700 firefighters battled the flames. Temperatures and winds are expected to peak tonight, complicating containment efforts.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
stories.summary: A Saudi oil tanker was attacked by Yemen's Houthi forces in the Red Sea, causing a fire, as the US launched its 12th consecutive night of strikes on Iran. The attack occurred despite US President Donald Trump's warning that the US would inflict "major military punishment" on the Houthis and Iran if they continued such actions. Oil prices surged to $100 per barrel following the latest developments in the region.

stories.title: Gary Lineker joins calls for wealth tax - The Latest
stories.summary: More than 100 UK millionaires, including former footballer Gary Lineker, have signed an open letter urging Prime Minister Andy Burnham to introduce a wealth tax. The signatories say they can afford higher taxes and want the government to act. Campaigners argue the move could ease public spending pressures amid rising costs.

stories.title: Chris Sale slated to start series finale versus San Diego
stories.summary: Braves ace Chris Sale is set to start the series finale against the San Diego Padres, looking to help the team win a series at home for the first time since 2018. Sale has been impressive this season, boasting a 10-6 record and a 2.06 ERA, and he recently threw a gem against the Texas Rangers with seven shutout innings. The Braves have a chance to split the series with a win, which would be a significant accomplishment given their rollercoaster rotation this season.

stories.title: Amazon puts Luna cloud-streamed games like Fallout 4 inside Prime Video
stories.summary: Amazon’s Prime Video now lets Fire TV users in the US and UK stream cloud games like Fallout 4 directly through a new Games tab. The feature bundles Prime Gaming, Amazon Game Studios, and Luna under one casual-friendly hub. More titles and regions are expected later this year.

stories.title: Oscar Voters Rave Over Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ at First Academy Screenings: ‘The Definitive Filmmaker of Our Time’
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey kicked off its Oscar campaign with exclusive member screenings this week, drawing near-capacity crowds in Los Angeles, London, and New York. The $264 million blockbuster now leads the 2027 race as the film to beat. Academy voters will cast ballots in the fall.

stories.title: ‘Let’s get back to work’: Karoline Leavitt returns to the briefing room podium for the first time since maternity leave
stories.summary: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt returned to the podium on Thursday, giving her first full briefing since maternity leave at the end of April. Leavitt addressed a range of topics including President Donald Trump's upcoming speech on election integrity, Iran, and the economy. She emphasized the importance of dedication and perseverance as a working mom, crediting her husband for supporting her.

stories.title: Athletes and Execs Join Forces at Variety’s Inaugural Sports and Entertainment Studio, Featuring Russell Wilson, Jimmie Johnson and Jay Williams
stories.summary: Variety hosted its first Sports & Entertainment Studio in New York on July 16, featuring Russell Wilson, Jimmie Johnson and Jay Williams alongside executives from State Farm, PepsiCo and Teads. The event explored how sports stars and brands can collaborate to engage fans during a booming media landscape. Wilson, a Super Bowl champion and entrepreneur, discussed his dual roles in sports and business.

stories.title: Business rate cut not enough, says Michelin-star chef
stories.summary: A 20% cut in business rates for pubs and live music venues from April will do little to ease the financial strain on independent restaurants, warns Michelin-starred chef Alex Claridge. Operating on thin margins despite full dining rooms, many independents remain unprofitable nationwide. The industry warns further support is needed to prevent closures.

stories.title: Anderson gets Man City move he craved as £116m deal done
stories.summary: Manchester City have completed the £116 million signing of midfielder Joachim Anderson from Nottingham Forest. The deal, a club record, sees Anderson join a side now managed by Enzo Maresca following Pep Guardiola’s departure. City confirmed the move ahead of the new Premier League season.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel to secure a U.S. nuclear energy deal. The agreement, which bars uranium enrichment, hinges on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords. Saudi Arabia has previously linked normalization to a Palestinian state but faces a White House ultimatum.

stories.title: Gary Lineker among millionaires asking Andy Burnham to tax them more
stories.summary: Gary Lineker and 119 other wealthy Britons have urged Prime Minister Andy Burnham to raise taxes on the richest, proposing a 2% levy on fortunes over £10 million. The Patriotic Millionaires group argues this would fund public services and reduce inequality. The Treasury already accepts voluntary donations from individuals willing to pay more.

stories.title: Colman Domingo Rips Elon Musk’s Plan for AI ‘The Odyssey’ Movie: ‘It’ll Be ‘Whiter Than Christmas at Cracker Barrel’
stories.summary: Colman Domingo criticized Elon Musk’s AI-generated “The Odyssey” movie plan, calling it “whiter than Christmas at Cracker Barrel.” Domingo made the remarks during his guest hosting spot on “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Wednesday. Musk’s team has faced backlash over diversity casting in the project.

stories.title: Trump Prepares Yet Another Strategy to Resurrect Global Tariffs
stories.summary: President Trump’s administration plans to replace a lapsing 10 percent global tariff with new 10 to 12.5 percent tariffs on 59 countries and the European Union. The new tariffs, set to take effect by Friday, target goods linked to forced labor under Section 301 of the 1974 Trade Act. Additional duties are expected to follow in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Microsoft is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming for Xbox Insiders
stories.summary: Microsoft is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming for Xbox Insiders today. Sessions are capped at one hour with ads only before play begins, and participation is optional for those with paid subscriptions. The limited-time test aims to explore more affordable ways to access Xbox games.

stories.title: Super Giants thrash dismal Spirit at Lord's
stories.summary: Manchester Super Giants crushed London Spirit by seven wickets at Lord's on Friday. Super Giants chased 81 in just 59 balls, with Amy Scholfield top-scoring on 22. The win marks Super Giants' first match under their new name after five years as Manchester Originals.

stories.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
stories.summary: Former Kenyan governor Ferdinand Waititu was convicted of orchestrating the 2018 murder of pregnant student Sharon Otieno. Three co-defendants, including his former aide, were also found guilty and remanded ahead of sentencing. Investigators traced the abductors’ vehicle using its hidden tracking device.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: A new UK-wide crackdown on petrol pump theft starts today, requiring drivers to pay upfront or leave a card deposit before fueling. Police report a 15% rise in drive-offs this year, costing forecourts over £50 million annually. Forecourt owners urge customers to carry cash or cards to avoid delays.

stories.title: Rangers sign 'huge potential' in Partizan captain Dragojevic
stories.summary: Rangers have signed Serbia midfielder Vanja Dragojevic from Partizan Belgrade on a four-year deal with a one-year option. The 20-year-old captained Partizan and has two international caps. Manager Derek McInnes called him a high-potential leader with exciting attributes.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: The US military will begin screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency. Those with low levels will be offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy to maintain force readiness. The initiative aims to ensure a healthy and capable fighting force.

stories.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
stories.summary: NASA’s MAVEN mission revealed Mars experiences auroras through the same particle process as Earth, though on a smaller scale due to weaker magnetic fields. Findings published Thursday in Nature Communications show the Dungey cycle drives these auroras after charged particles reconnect with the Sun’s magnetic field. Data from the now-concluded mission continues to guide future Mars exploration efforts.

stories.title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
stories.summary: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.

stories.title: Chaos Breaks Out at Border Crossings in Iraq
stories.summary: Videos verified by The New York Times show border crossings in southern Iraq becoming the latest target in the hostilities in the Middle East.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
stories.summary: Crews at NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans moved the 130-foot Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank to a test building on May 15, 2026. The tank will power the SLS rocket’s core stage with super-cold propellant for four RS-25 engines. Testing is expected to follow before integration into the final rocket assembly.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study in JAMA Neurology finds that long-term exposure to road traffic noise may increase Parkinson’s disease risk by 3% for every 11.5-decibel rise in noise levels. Researchers tracked 3.1 million Danish adults over 18 years, linking higher noise exposure at home to a modest but consistent rise in cases. Experts suggest reducing noise at home could help lower the risk.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.
